description: Marketing CDN facts and legacy fallbacks. Homepage hero carousel: `src/config/home-hero-carousel.ts` — `screenshot1.png` … `screenshot15.png` at CDN root (nursenest-images.tor1.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com).

homepageHeroCarousel.notes: Canonical mapping and captions: `src/config/home-hero-carousel.ts`. Public URL pattern: {digitalOceanSpaces.nursenestImages.publicBaseUrl}/screenshot{N}.png. Default: direct HTTPS; optional /api/marketing-assets/... when NEXT_PUBLIC_MARKETING_USE_SPACES_PROXY=true or FALLBACK=true (see marketing-resolve-image-url.ts).

nursenestImages.notes: Prefer CDN hostname `*.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com` for public assets. Private buckets: set NEXT_PUBLIC_MARKETING_USE_SPACES_PROXY=true and SPACES_KEY/SPACES_SECRET so /api/marketing-assets/... can stream objects.
nursenestImages.cachingNotes: This Next.js app cannot set response headers on cross-origin CDN URLs. Set recommendedCacheControl on Spaces object metadata and/or CDN edge rules so direct CDN image responses are long-cached. Same-origin /api/marketing-assets/* already sends this Cache-Control on 200. next/image uses images.minimumCacheTTL (see next.config.ts) for optimized fetches.

committedMarketingAssets.notes: Homepage screenshot URLs in committed marketing-assets.generated.ts use digitalOceanSpaces.nursenestImages.publicBaseUrl + /screenshots/ (same paths as legacy site). Logo may still use legacyFallbackUrl until SPACES discovery runs.

logo.notes: Per-theme logo keys: `src/config/theme-brand-logo-space-keys.ts` (explicit filenames). Resolver uses proxy-first via `getThemeLogoLoadChain`. Keep `primaryBrandMarkThemeTinted` false. Generic blue mark is last resort after legacy GIF/SVG.
logo.discoveryFilenamePattern: Objects whose key matches /logo/i (see scripts/generate-marketing-assets.mjs CATEGORY_TESTS.LOGO_PRIMARY).

lessonImages.inNursenestCore: No static DigitalOcean Spaces map. Lesson media is not hard-coded in this package.
lessonImages.notes: The main application server resolves stored object_path values to this relative API path (see server/routes.ts). Bucket id comes from deployment env (e.g. Replit object storage), not from marketing-cdn.catalog.json.
